content: Root .gitignore should preserve git-native project folders (.canopy, .mulch, .seeds, .overstory metadata) and ignore only local runtime files plus standard Electron/Node build artifacts.

name: linear-team-lookup
description: Linear workspace currently exposes a team named 'Ulra-agentic-ide' (note the missing 't') and no standalone project named 'ultra-agentic-ide'; issue lookups for this work should target the team.

name: linear-project-created
description: Created Linear project 'ultra agentic ide' and associated it with the existing team 'Ulra-agentic-ide' (team key ULR).

name: execution-backlog-mapping
description: The implementation-plan, contract, schema, and wiring docs map cleanly to a six-milestone execution backlog; the only execution-blocking spikes are Overstory coordinator control-contract definition, Code-OSS embedding inside Electron, and manual-browser embedding with isolated automation sessions.

title: First chat adapter uses OpenAI Responses API
rationale: Ultra v1 ships ChatRuntimeAdapter first against the OpenAI Responses API, defaulting new chats to gpt-5.2 and offering gpt-5.1-codex-max as an optional coding-focused preset when available. Thinking controls map directly to model-supported reasoning effort values, while supervised/full_access remains an Ultra-owned execution policy rather than a model switch.

title: Editor, browser, and GitHub integration boundaries
rationale: Ultra v1 embeds Code-OSS in a dedicated Electron WebContentsView behind an EditorHostAdapter, keeps the manual browser on a persistent WebContentsView partition, runs automation QA in Playwright with thread-scoped isolated profiles, and uses a user-supplied GitHub PAT stored only in the OS keychain for draft PR creation. These choices keep secrets, session state, and workflow ownership inside clear boundaries.

title: Coordinator NDJSON control contract
rationale: Ultra v1 treats each project coordinator as a backend-supervised child process and uses newline-delimited JSON over stdin/stdout for hello, ping, start_thread, send_thread_message, retry, pause/resume, and structured runtime/thread events. This keeps process supervision and all SQLite writes inside the backend while coordinators remain stateless from the product database perspective.

title: ChatRuntimeAdapter wraps coding CLIs over stdio
rationale: Ultra v1 does not use ChatRuntimeAdapter as a direct vendor API adapter. Instead, it wraps local codex CLI and claude code CLI processes, supervises them from the backend, and communicates primarily over stdio while normalizing outputs into Ultra chat/thread models. The default OpenAI model target in the Codex path is GPT-5.4.

name: linear-backlog-aligned-to-locked-architecture
description: Updated the Ultra Linear backlog so the runtime adapter, coordinator contract, Code-OSS embedding, browser isolation, and GitHub publish-auth issues reflect the locked v1 decisions rather than open questions. Key updated issues: ULR-17, ULR-18, ULR-29, ULR-36, ULR-40, ULR-46, ULR-56, and ULR-61.

name: linear-dependency-cleanup-after-architecture-lock
description: After locking the runtime, editor, browser, coordinator, and publish-auth decisions, reviewed the related Linear dependency graph. Existing blockers were already explicit for ULR-29, ULR-36, ULR-46, ULR-56, and ULR-61; added the missing direct blocker from ULR-18 to ULR-20 so direct coding checkpoints explicitly wait on the CLI-backed runtime adapter.

name: boundary-docs-before-implementation
description: For cross-cutting implementation seams that are not large enough for new milestone architecture docs, add short top-level boundary specs instead. Ultra now has  for CLI-runtime/stdin-stdout integration and  for Code-OSS/manual-browser ownership boundaries, with links from the product spec and implementation-plan entrypoints.

name: ulr5-workspace-scaffold
description: ULR-5 scaffolds Ultra as a pnpm workspace with apps/desktop on Electron+Vite+React, apps/backend on Node+TypeScript, packages/shared for shared contracts, and pnpm build-script approval for electron/esbuild via root package metadata.

electron-vite emits the preload bundle as dist/preload/index.mjs by default; pointing BrowserWindow.webPreferences.preload at index.js causes a white screen and an undefined preload bridge in the renderer.
resolution: Point the preload path at dist/preload/index.mjs, and keep a defensive renderer fallback so missing preload state does not hard-crash the shell.

name: electron-vite-preload-stable-js
description: Configure electron-vite preload builds to emit dist/preload/index.js with CommonJS output so BrowserWindow.webPreferences.preload uses a stable path in dev and build mode.
Electron preload bundles built as CommonJS cannot use a .js filename inside a package marked type=module; Electron will try to treat the preload as ESM and fail with require-is-not-defined.
resolution: Emit the preload bundle as dist/preload/index.cjs and point BrowserWindow.webPreferences.preload at that .cjs file.

name: ulr6-app-shell-mode-router
description: ULR-6 establishes the renderer shell with a small Zustand app store, top-pill mode navigation for Chat/Editor/Browser, and a page router that keeps all page shells mounted while hiding inactive ones so project-scoped UI state is preserved during page switches.

name: app-shell-sharp-dark-baseline
description: The Ultra app shell baseline uses a near-black background, square-cornered surfaces, and a compact top-pill mode switcher rather than soft rounded cards, to keep the desktop chrome crisp and tool-like while preserving the dark-only shell policy.

name: sharp-shell-pill-nav
description: The Ultra shell uses sharp-edged header cards and content surfaces, while the primary mode switcher for Chat/Editor/Browser remains rounded and pill-like even when the surrounding shell geometry is squared off.

name: ignore-generated-biome-paths
description: Root Biome includes should explicitly ignore generated .cache and dist folders under apps/ and packages/ so workspace lint stays stable after build outputs are produced.

name: shared-contract-layer
description: ULR-8 turns @ultra/shared into the contract package for M1 by defining Zod-validated IPC wire envelopes in snake_case and shared system, project, and layout DTOs in camelCase so backend and renderer can validate the same shapes without bespoke mapping.

name: shared-dist-exports-for-runtime
description: Workspace packages that are externalized at runtime, like @ultra/shared in Electron main/preload and the Node backend, must export built dist artifacts rather than raw src TypeScript; root dev should also keep shared in watch mode so those runtime consumers see up-to-date JS.

name: root-dev-should-not-double-run-backend
description: Once the desktop shell owns backend startup, the root dev command should run only the shared watcher and desktop dev process; starting the backend separately at the root causes duplicate backend processes and hides whether Electron lifecycle ownership is working.

name: desktop-owned-backend-supervisor
description: ULR-7 keeps backend lifecycle ownership in Electron main: spawn a separate Node backend child, expose status to preload/renderer through shell IPC, and map backend phases into the renderer's connecting/connected/degraded/disconnected state instead of letting the renderer infer process health itself.

title: Treat backend connection as protocol-ready only after system.hello
rationale: Desktop-owned backend supervision is necessary but not sufficient for connection health. Ultra should report the backend as connected only after a validated system.hello handshake succeeds over the Unix socket, so renderer state reflects actual protocol compatibility instead of mere child-process liveness.

description: Electron-managed backend startup in this workspace failed when desktop code assumed a root-level node_modules/.bin/tsx or used tsx watch for the child process. In the worktree, the reliable executable was apps/backend/node_modules/.bin/tsx, and plain tsx src/index.ts produced a deterministic child the desktop process manager could supervise.
resolution: Resolve the backend dev launch path from the backend package directory and spawn the package-local tsx binary with src/index.ts instead of tsx watch. This keeps backend ownership with the Electron supervisor and avoids ENOENT or unstable child-process behavior.

title: Bootstrap SQLite before socket startup using a desktop-owned database path
rationale: Ultra's backend should not accept IPC requests until local persistence is ready. The backend now requires ULTRA_DB_PATH from the desktop launcher, opens the database, applies pragmas and migrations, and only then starts the Unix socket server. This keeps startup failure modes honest and lets later project/layout services depend on a ready database instead of bootstrapping lazily behind request handlers.

description: Using better-sqlite3 for the first persistence slice introduced native-install friction in this workspace because pnpm suppressed the package's build/install script, leaving the backend without a usable SQLite binding during tests.
resolution: Use Node's built-in node:sqlite DatabaseSync API for the M1 persistence foundation. It keeps the backend pure Node, removes native module packaging friction from the Electron workflow, and still supports the required local-file bootstrap, pragmas, and migration flow.

name: git-root-backed project identity
description: For project-open flows, Ultra should canonicalize the opened directory with realpath, detect a git top-level directory when present, and use . This keeps nested folders inside one repo mapped to a single durable project row while still preserving the last opened directory in .

name: git-root-backed project identity
description: For project-open flows, Ultra should canonicalize the opened directory with realpath, detect a git top-level directory when present, and use project_key = git_root_path or root_path. This keeps nested folders inside one repo mapped to a single durable project row while still preserving the last opened directory in root_path.

title: Keep ULR-11 backend-first with a thin desktop bridge
rationale: ULR-11 should make projects.open, projects.get, and projects.list real backend operations and expose them through Electron main and preload, but it should not hydrate renderer state or update the shell project frame yet. That keeps project identity and IPC honest without collapsing the larger renderer/store work from ULR-12 into this ticket.

title: Preserve the generic desktop IPC bridge when layering project-open support
rationale: When ULR-11 project-open work rebased onto ULR-12, the correct integration was to keep the generic Electron main/preload bridge (`ipcQuery` and `ipcCommand`) introduced by ULR-12 and expose project operations through that path, rather than keeping a second set of preload-specific project methods. This avoids duplicate desktop APIs while preserving the new backend project methods.

name: visible-project-open-uses-generic-ipc
description: For visible project opening in the desktop shell, keep the generic ipcQuery/ipcCommand bridge as the data path and add only one dedicated preload primitive for directory selection. The shell should use a folder picker, projects.open, recent-project hydration from projects.list, and activeProjectId updates in the renderer store rather than adding a second project-specific transport layer.

title: Gate environment readiness after handshake
rationale: Ultra should query environment readiness after system.hello, block app entry when required CLI tools are unavailable, and keep readiness UI in a startup blocker plus Settings > System & Tools rather than the shell header or runtime indicator.

name: environment-readiness-tool-matrix
description: Environment readiness probes PATH-based CLIs with explicit version commands, treats sd as a runtime-required dependency alongside ov/tmux/git/codex/claude, and only enforces minimum versions for node and pnpm from the repo engines and packageManager contract.

title: Use a dedicated foundation startup gate for core boot failures
rationale: Backend launch failures, handshake failures, and database bootstrap or migration failures should block entry into the normal shell, while project-open failures remain inline in the project frame. This keeps startup breakage visible without turning all action errors into modal blockers.

name: Lifecycle-only chat IPC with internal reads
description: Expose only chats.create/list/get/rename/pin/unpin/archive/restore in the public IPC surface while keeping ChatService internal read helpers for sessions, messages, and refs so later transcript and thread features reuse the same SQL layer without schema churn.

title: Prebuild shared before backend package execution
rationale: Backend-local runtime and test commands resolve @ultra/shared through package dist exports, so backend package scripts that execute code should prebuild the shared package to avoid stale contract artifacts.

name: non-blocking supervised cli runtime mappings
description: For ULR-18, supervised chat turns must remain unattended: Codex maps supervised/full_access to workspace-write/danger-full-access, Claude maps them to auto/bypassPermissions, and adapter tests should normalize stdout into assistant/tool/checkpoint events rather than exposing raw CLI transcripts as product state.

title: CLI chat runtimes use logical session reuse over per-turn child processes
rationale: ULR-18 keeps Codex and Claude runtime continuity through backend-owned in-memory session records and vendor resume/session-id support, but each turn still launches a supervised stdio process so Ultra does not depend on undocumented always-on interactive daemons. If resume fails, the backend invalidates the session and retries once from persisted chat context.

name: coordinator events project through backend-owned persistence
description: For ULR-36, coordinator responses only acknowledge commands while durable state changes come from coordinator events that the backend projects into threads, thread_events, thread_messages, thread_agents, project_runtimes, runtime_components, and runtime_health_checks. Coordinators and watchdogs never write Ultra SQLite directly.

title: Coordinator contract uses per-instance NDJSON ordering with stable project identity
rationale: ULR-36 locks the coordinator transport to NDJSON over supervised child stdio with snake_case envelopes, a stable per-project coordinator_id, and a restart-scoped coordinator_instance_id whose sequence_number space resets on each restart. This gives RuntimeSupervisor and recovery code a clear deduplication and replay boundary without changing thread identity.

name: runtime-registry-foundation-slice
description: ULR-37 should land runtime persistence as a backend-only foundation slice: add shared runtime DTOs plus a SQLite-backed RuntimePersistenceService and hydrated RuntimeRegistry first, keep project_runtimes on doc-level keys while referencing the shipped projects.id primary key, and leave public runtime.* IPC routes for ULR-42.

title: Pivot roadmap to chat-plus-terminal workflow
rationale: The core user problem is reducing the feedback loop between AI-generated changes and local testing. A worktree-aware terminal with env sync solves that more directly and with far less complexity than embedded editor and browser surfaces.

name: linear-backlog-pivot-worktree-terminal
description: Linear backlog realignment after the chat-plus-terminal pivot: M3 renamed to Worktree Terminal Workflow, M5 renamed to External QA and Artifact Handoff, M3 tickets ULR-28/30-35/74 repurposed around worktrees and integrated terminal flow, and obsolete embedded editor/browser spikes ULR-29/46/48/49/51/52/56/76/78 plus ULR-78 were canceled.

name: direction-migration-closeout
description: Close a major product-direction pivot by updating the roadmap, lower-level contract and wiring docs, and the active backlog together before new implementation work resumes. For the chat-first worktree-terminal pivot, all three layers now speak the same model.

title: Hide worktrees behind sandboxes in a multi-project chat shell
rationale: Ultra's v1 UX should center on multiple projects in the sidebar, chats under each project, a right-side thread panel, and a chat-page terminal drawer. Threads remain Overstory-backed by default, but users should interact through sandbox selection and terminal workflow instead of editor/browser pages or raw worktree concepts.

name: sandbox-foundation-preserves-hidden-active-id
description: ULR-74 lands sandbox persistence as a backend-only foundation slice: migration 0006 adds sandbox_contexts, project_runtime_profiles, sandbox_runtime_syncs, and a hidden project_layout_state.last_active_sandbox_id; ProjectService.setLayout must use conflict-upsert semantics so public layout writes do not erase the active sandbox linkage.

name: thread-creation-transaction
description: ULR-25 creates threads through one SQLite transaction that inserts thread snapshot/link rows, appends a thread.created event to thread_events, and projects last_event_sequence from the durable event stream before commit.

name: sandbox-public-ipc-wraps-foundation
description: ULR-28 keeps sandbox selection narrow by wrapping SandboxPersistenceService with a thin SandboxService and exposing only sandboxes.list, sandboxes.get_active, and sandboxes.set_active over IPC; runtime sync visibility stays deferred to terminal/runtime tickets.

name: merge-backend-ipc-slices-on-main
description: When landing backend slices that both touch IPC wiring, merge them by unioning router methods, backend service injection, shared contract exports, and socket coverage, then rerun the backend/shared suite on main; thread message seeding should use ChatService.appendMessage rather than a separate helper API.

title: Terminal-owned runtime sync
rationale: This keeps sandbox selection APIs stable for the shell while letting terminal flows own runtime profile and managed-copy sync state.

title: PTY-backed terminal sessions
rationale: This keeps terminal lifecycle in the backend, makes Open Terminal a real sandbox-aware operation, and gives the drawer a stable live session/output model without depending on editor embedding.

name: chat-first desktop shell hydration
description: ULR-30 makes the desktop shell chat-first by normalizing restored layout state back to chat, persisting terminal drawer visibility through the existing bottom-pane layout fields, and hydrating sandboxes, runtime status, and terminal sessions per active project.

name: ulr30-rebase-shell-unification
description: When rebasing the ULR-30 shell onto newer mainline renderer work, keep the project-scoped sandboxes/terminal store and let AppShell delegate shell controls to ChatPageShell; older sandbox selector/workflow helpers must be adapted or removed so the renderer only has one sandbox state model.

name: artifact-migrations-foundation-slice
description: ULR-77 should remain migration-only: add 0008_artifacts_and_sharing with artifacts and artifact_shares plus FK/index coverage in migrator/bootstrap tests, and leave artifact DTOs, storage services, and artifacts.* IPC to later M5 tickets.

title: Thread UX centers one coordinator conversation
rationale: Ultra should present each thread as one durable coordinator conversation with collapsible thread cards, a single expanded detail view, and worker or swarm activity kept secondary in diagnostics tabs. This keeps Overstory hidden behind the product boundary while preserving the chat-to-thread-to-review feedback loop.

content: Cmd+K renderer and terminal command-generation tests should use full backend-compatible Claude model identifiers (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6) instead of short aliases, to keep provider/model defaults and payload expectations aligned with backend CLI argument construction.

name: verifier-cmdk-model-alignment
description: Verifier workflow for Cmd+K model/provider alignment: use linked task commit as scope anchor, confirm renderer defaults/options use full claude-* model names, and rerun package-scoped command-generation suites plus root typecheck for evidence.

name: cmdk-generate-hide-delta-json
description: Cmd+K renderer should not render terminal command-generation delta text because backend deltas are raw JSON fragments; keep generating UI spinner-only and preserve complete-event injection and error paths.

outcomes.test_results: pnpm --filter @ultra/desktop exec vitest run ... passed (2 files, 15 tests)

content: Verifier workflow for Cmd+K Codex path: run scoped backend and renderer Vitest suites; if codex CLI is missing, mark live Codex smoke as environment-blocked and rely on JSONL assistant.final parsing tests while separately validating Claude probe success.

name: sidebar-rename-inline-editor
description: Desktop sidebar chat rename should use inline chat-row editing (not window.prompt) and only submit trimmed, non-empty, changed titles; Escape/blank/unchanged values should no-op to keep rename stable in Electron.
Verifier finding: apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/sidebar/sidebar.test.tsx currently fails due stale expectations unrelated to commit 47d5040 (expects outdated sidebar copy/expansion behavior).
resolution: For rename verification, run targeted suites: sidebar-rename.test.ts, chat-row.test.tsx, and chat-workflows.test.ts; treat sidebar.test.tsx updates as separate maintenance work.

name: chat-page-active-pane-shell
description: ULR-16 active chat pane shell is renderer-only: replace ChatPageShell placeholder with transcript/references/input-dock structure while preserving activeChat derivation from layout+sidebar and leaving message transport wiring to ULR-19.
